title: camissonia cardiophylla subsp. cedrosensis
intro: Camissonia cardiophylla subsp. cedrosensis, also known as San Clemente Island evening primrose, is a rare, endangered species of flowering plant native to the island of San Clemente off the coast of California, USA. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Camissonia cardiophylla subsp. cedrosensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and aid in wound healing, as well as its use as an antioxidant and anti-cancer agent. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures made from the plant's flowers and leaves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
